data.title: Qualigen Therapeutics regains compliance with Nasdaq's listing requirements
data.description:
• Qualigen Therapeutics (NASDAQ:QLGN) received formal notice from Nasdaq that the company has regained compliance with Nasdaq’s minimum bid price requirement and stockholders’ equity requirement.
  • The company will be subject to a mandatory panel monitor in connection with the equity requirement for a period of one year from December 2, 2024.
  • QLGN shares were down 4.7% on Thursday.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Damon names Dominique Kwong as interim CEO
data.description:
• Damon (NASDAQ:DMN) announced the appointment of Damon co-founder, Dominique Kwong as interim chief executive officer, and Shashi Tripathi as chairman of the board.
  • Kwong assumes the interim CEO role after first serving on the company's board and as chief technology officer and then subsequently its chief operating officer between 2017 and 2023. 
  • Kwong replaces Jay Giraud as CEO.
  • DMN shares were down 2.9% on Thursday. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nano Labs enters strategic Partnership with HashKey Group for Web3 innovation
data.description:
• Nano Labs (NASDAQ:NA) has entered into a strategic cooperate agreement to explore in-depth cooperation in the Web3 space and blockchain ecosystem with HashKey Group.
  • This partnership marks a significant step in Nano Labs's commitment to advancing the technical innovation and application in Web3 and blockchain ecosystem.
  • Pursuant to the Agreement, a subsidiary of Nano Labs will open a trading account on HashKey Exchange, a licensed virtual asset trading platform in Hong Kong under HashKey Group, and invest in virtual assets to diversify its portfolio in the digital currency sector.
  • The collaboration extends beyond digital asset trading and is expected to include Web3 technology development and blockchain ecosystem construction, fostering innovation and growth in the Web3 industry.

data.title: Elevance under scrutiny over anesthesia coverage change for some Anthem plans
data.description:
Elevance Health (NYSE:ELV) has drawn the ire of a major physicians group and the public over a policy change slated to go into effect on Feb. 1 for Anthem members in New York, Connecticut, and Missouri that would limit the amount of anethesia and anesthetic services covered during surgeries and procedures.

According to an Anthem provider newsletter, the insurer will now use the CMS Physician Work Time values as the basis to determine appropriate anesthesia coverage. "Claims submitted with reported time above the established number of minutes will be denied." However, individuals under 22 and maternity-related care are exempt from the new policy.

The American Society of Anesthesiologists is opposed to the change and sent a letter to Elevance CEO Gail Boudreau in November requesting a meeting.

"Not only is the use of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services Physician Work Time Value not an accepted, reliable, or rational method for payment, it demonstrates Anthem’s lack of knowledge regarding how anesthesia payments are made," the letter reads. "The CMS Physician Work Time was not developed nor intended to support reporting or payment for anesthesia services."

Calling the move a "cynical money grab by Anthem," ASA President Donald Arnold said in a news release, “This is just the latest in a long line of appalling behavior by commercial health insurers looking to drive their profits up at the expense of patients and physicians providing essential care."

Seeking Alpha has reached out to Elevance Health for comment but has yet to hear back.

data.title: American Eagle Outfitters downgraded at J.P. Morgan as markdowns presents a 'notable risk'
data.description:
American Eagle Outfitters (NYSE:AEO) limped into Thursday’s open with a double-digit loss as the retailer’s drop in sales and lowered full year outlook led J.P. Morgan to downgrade the stock to Neutral from Overweight.

Shares were down 16% at the open and wiped-out last week’s gains.

The reversal in the stock was triggered by disappointing Q3 results which showed a 1% drop in sales, slowing comparable sales into its Aerie and American Eagle businesses, and lowered sales guidance for the year.

“While long-term, we see AEO driving growth across the brand portfolio across both AE and Aerie and operating margin expansion on fixed expense leverage, we see recent moderating sales and consumer ‘choppiness’ experienced over the trailing 6 months contributing to inconsistent results and execution risk with markdowns a notable potential risk,” J.P. Morgan’s Matthew Boss said. Boss also cut his target price by 15% to $23, a 12% upside to Wednesday’s closing price.

The company’s creative director acknowledge the “choppiness” in non-peak periods and less consistency in its direct business during the company’s earnings call to explain the revision to Q4 comparable sales outlook.

"Moving into Holiday, we're seeing a little bit of some choppiness just in a couple of weeks, we just want to be really cautious, and we want to deliver strong results," said Jennifer Foyle.

Decelerating same store sales is a “red flag” for Boss as he sees management’s expectations for sequential comparable sales improvement problematic considering December comps coming in 200 basis points “tougher” relative to November.

Boss also lowered his adjusted earnings estimates for FY24 and FY25 to $1.66 and $1.87 from $1.78 and $2.05, respectively, versus the consensus estimates of $1.68 for FY25 and $1.89 for FY26.

The selling pressure on American Eagle (AEO) is spilling over into comparable retailers with Abercrombie & Fitch (ANF) down 5%, Gap (GAP) lower by 1.6%, and shares of Urban Outfitters (URBN) down 2.5%.

data.title: The Tech mega-caps will lead again in 2025 – UBS
data.description:
Although consensus expects the gap between the mega-cap technology stocks with the rest of the market to narrow, the big stocks will continue to lead next year.

The largest six stocks in the S&P 500 (SP500) by market cap — Apple (AAPL), Nvidia (NVDA), Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), Alphabet Class A (GOOGL), Alphabet Class C (GOOG) — have returned 149% since 2023, with EPS growing 93%. Also, 76% of the upside for the biggest six stocks can be attributed to earnings, compared to 11% for the non-technology stocks.

EPS is expected to grow 28% for the technology stocks (NYSEARCA:XLK), (IYW), (VGT) this year, compared to 4% for the rest of the market, although the gap is expected to narrow to 10.5% in 2025, with technology stocks growing 20% compared to 9.5% for the rest of the market.

“The overall growth number for technology (XLK) obfuscates differences between the ‘Big 6’ and the remainder of the group,” wrote Jonathan Golub, chief U.S. equity strategist at UBS, in a note. “In 2024, for example, ‘Big 6’ EPS expanded 38%, versus 12% for the rest of the tech sector (XLK), a spread of 26%. This gap is expected to close in 2025 with ‘Big 6’ growth modestly lagging the rest of the group, 19% vs. 21.5%.”

Revisions have also been dominated by the largest six stocks for 2024-2025, the rest of the technology sector (XLK) “is only modestly ahead of historical averages,” said Golub.

data.title: Petrobras, Ecopetrol tout Colombia's largest ever gas discovery
data.description:
Petrobras (NYSE:PBR) and partner Ecopetrol (EC) confirmed on Thursday the largest gas discovery in the history of Colombia, with the drilling of the Sirius-2 well in the Gujaira Basin.

Petrobras (PBR) said in a securities filing that drilling results confirmed gas volumes of more than 6T cf in place at the site, which could increase Colombia's current reserves by 200%.

The consortium, 44.4% owned by operator Petrobras with Ecopetrol (EC) owning a 55.5% stake, will begin acquiring meta-oceanic data as part of the production development project for the discovery, the companies said.

The companies expect to start natural gas production within three years after obtaining all environmental licenses and upon confirmation of the commercial viability of the discovery, expected by 2027.

data.title: Oppenheimer Asset Management places Buy ratings on AVGO and BLDR and a Sell call on EW
data.description:
Oppenheimer Asset Management came out on Thursday and detailed two buying opportunities and one selling opportunity according to its technical and fundamental research.

The investment institution placed a Buy call on both Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) and shares of Builders FirstSource (NYSE:BLDR) while also placing a Sell call on Edwards Lifesciences Corporation (NYSE:EW).

Outlined below is what Oppenheimer Asset Management had to say about AVGO, BLDR, and EW along with some general market stats and Seeking Alpha quant metrics on each of the stocks.

Broadcom (AVGO)

“AVGO is inflecting positively the bullish slope of its smoothed trend which we view as a resumption of the stock’s long-term uptrend. We’re also positive on top-down tailwinds from a relatively strong large-cap growth style. We see $153 as key support (200-day average) to uphold and would view a rally above $185 resistance (June peak) as a resumption of the stock’s uptrend. “

  • AVGO currently trades at $169.85 a share.
  • AVGO is +52.1% year-to-date.
  • AVGO holds an SA quant raring of 3.45 and is listed as a Hold.

Builders FirstSource (BLDR)

“Despite BLDR’s mid-MO rank, we side positively towards the stock’s trend above $175 support (200-day average). A rally above $203 resistance (Sept. peak) could restart the stock’s uptrend and, in turn, support its MO score over the coming months, in our view.”

  • BLDR currently trades at $178.13 a share.
  • BLDR is +6.8% year-to-date.
  • BLDR holds an SA quant raring of 2.86 and is listed as a Hold.

Edwards Lifesciences Corporation (EW)

“EW has rallied since July but the stock remains below the bearish slope of its smoothed trend. Trading maintains an upward drift above $68 support (50-day average) though we prefer to sell strength into $79 resistance (200-day average) based on the stock’s low-MO score.”

  • EW currently trades at $73.33 a share.
  • EW is -3.8% year-to-date.
  • EW holds an SA quant raring of 2.81 and is listed as a Hold.

data.title: TAT Technologies rated Buy in new coverage at Benchmark
data.description:
TAT Technologies (NASDAQ:TATT) on Thursday was rated as a Buy among analysts at financial-services firm Benchmark. They said the provider of what are known as aftermarket services for the aviation industry is poised for growth.

“TAT’s (TATT) aerospace aftermarket heritage spans seven decades, and the company is an industry leader in thermal management solutions and power and actuation services,” Josh Sullivan, analyst at Benchmark, said in a December 5 report. “However, the company has flown under the radar despite the overall market interest in aerospace aftermarket players.”

During the pandemic, TAT (TATT) signed several 10-year deals with Honeywell International (HON) to provide maintenance services for auxiliary power units, which provide power energy for other functions than aircraft propulsion.

“The Honeywell (HON) strategic APU relationships, while signed in 2020/2021, took time to receive certifications as well as ramp capacity, which only occurred fully this year,” according to Benchmark. “Then lastly, TAT (TATT) recently moved the center of operations from Israel to North Carolina, which will help with U.S. investor exposure and airline relationships.”

Benchmark set a price target of $30 on TAT (TATT), based on an estimated enterprise value that’s 18.5 times earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization for full-year 2024.

Shares of TAT (TATT) rose 4% on Thursday morning. The small-cap stock has more than doubled in value in the past 12 months.

data.title: Optimism among retail investors rises in latest AAII survey
data.description:
Investors’ optimism about the short-term outlook for the stock market increased during the week, showed the latest AAII Sentiment survey.

A total of 48.3% of respondents, as per the AAII Sentiment survey, showed optimism, compared to 37.1% in the last week. This week’s figure is above the historical average of 37.5%.

The percentage of those surveyed who felt bearish also decreased, with the figure standing at 30.7% for the week ended Dec. 4, versus 38.6% last week.

This comes after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell made upbeat remarks about the U.S. economy, saying that it is stronger now than the central bank had expected in September and had flagged a slowdown in the job market as a factor in jump-starting its rate-cutting cycle this year.

Investors were also happy about earnings report from companies including Salesforce (CRM) and semiconductor firm Marvell Technology (MRVL).

However, the market also digested comments from President-elect Donald Trump as he threatened to implement more trade tariffs.

Neutral sentiment stood at 21% for the week, versus 24.3% last week, who saw no change in market direction.

The survey has been conducted by the American Association of Individual Investors (AAII) since 1987, in which it asks respondents for their thoughts on where the market is heading in the next six months.

The S&P 500 Index (SP500) rose nearly 1% in the last one week, while Dow Jones Industrial Average Index (DJI) gained marginally. NASDAQ Composite Index (COMP:IND) rose over 2% and NASDAQ 100-Index (NDX) gained nearly 3%.
